executive_summary: Child D of the intelligent op-co switch-on automation orchestrator — the integration child that wires children A/B/C into a real production decision path and applies it to a real fenced op-co component. Child A shipped classifySwitchOn() + the chairman-ratified NEVER-AUTO class list; Child B shipped authorizeSwitchOn(), a pure enforcing consumer with zero production callers; Child C shipped the 7 mandatory prechecks. All three are built-but-not-wired. This SD wires authorizeSwitchOn() (plus Child C's prechecks) into the EXISTING EXEC_BOUNDARY_HOLD PLAN-TO-EXEC gate (shipped by SD-LEO-INFRA-PHASE-SCOPED-FENCE-001), so a phase-scoped-fenced SD's PLAN-TO-EXEC handoff auto-clears on a reversible+precheck-green verdict, or fires a durable one-tap chairman decision packet (console record + best-effort SMS, reusing SD-LEO-FEAT-TWO-WAY-CHAIRMAN-001's sendChairmanSmsQuestion) on a consequential/unknown verdict instead of parking silently forever. Applies this concretely to SD-FDBK-ENH-EHG-OPERATING-COMPANY-001-A (provision the live venture surface), converting its blanket requires_human_action/do_not_auto_dispatch fence (which currently blocks LEAD/PLAN too, despite -A's own fence text saying LEAD/PLAN may proceed) to the phase-scoped exec_boundary_hold, unblocking LEAD/PLAN work immediately while EXEC correctly and durably escalates to the chairman (op-co-A's actions are NEVER-AUTO by GUARDRAIL-1's own named classes, so EXEC will never silently auto-proceed — that is the correct, intended outcome). Siblings -B and -C are explicitly OUT of scope: -B is chairman-PARKED for an unrelated demand-gating business reason (already once mis-corrected by the coordinator after a wrong unfence attempt), and -C depends on -B.

functional_requirements.title: Switch-on gate wired into the EXEC_BOUNDARY_HOLD PLAN-TO-EXEC gate
functional_requirements.description: Modify scripts/modules/handoff/executors/plan-to-exec/gates/exec-boundary-hold.js: when execBoundaryHoldReason(ctx.sd) returns a hold AND ctx.sd.metadata.switchon_action is a non-empty string, evaluate the switch-on gate before falling back to the existing manual-park WAIT. Evaluation calls classifySwitchOn (Child A) via authorizeSwitchOn (Child B) with {component: ctx.sd.sd_key, action: ctx.sd.metadata.switchon_action, reversible: ctx.sd.metadata.switchon_reversible, inRole: ctx.sd.metadata.switchon_in_role, isReversibleByMechanism: ctx.sd.metadata.switchon_reversible_by_mechanism, isLiveMoney: ctx.sd.metadata.switchon_is_live_money, isVentureCommitment: ctx.sd.metadata.switchon_is_venture_commitment} (VALIDATION F1: classifyDecision requires inRole===true for an EXECUTE/reversible verdict — switchon_in_role is a REQUIRED new metadata field in the data contract, its absence must map to inRole:undefined/null so the classifier's own uncertain-input handling applies, never a hard-coded true), and additionally runs Child C's runSwitchOnPrechecks() when authorizeSwitchOn returns authorized:true (a reversible verdict alone is not sufficient — the 7 mandatory prechecks must also be green). If BOTH authorized AND prechecks green: stamp metadata.exec_boundary_hold=false, exec_boundary_hold_cleared_by='switchon-gate-auto', exec_boundary_hold_cleared_at=<now>, exec_boundary_hold_auto_clear_evidence=<verdict+precheck summary>, and return {passed:true}. Otherwise (never-auto / consequential / unknown / prechecks red): call the FR-2 decision-packet dispatcher (idempotent) and return the SAME buildWaitResult shape the gate already returns today, with the verdict/precheck reasons folded into the wait details. When metadata.switchon_action is ABSENT, behavior is BYTE-IDENTICAL to today (pure manual park) — this is additive, not a replacement of the existing manual-clear path (a coordinator can still clear exec_boundary_hold by hand regardless). VALIDATION F2 (scope decision): runSwitchOnPrechecks needs a rich live evidence bundle (revertPath, observeClean, incidentEvidenceFn, gaugeProcessKey, rateSoakOpts, openIncident) with no production evidence-source wired anywhere yet, and no currently-fenced op-co component is actually reversible (op-co-A is permanently never-auto by its own named actions) — so the reversible+auto-clear path has NO real caller to exercise it in production today. This SD proves the auto-clear branch correct via unit tests with an explicitly STUBBED/mocked runSwitchOnPrechecks (TS-3) rather than wiring a live evidence source, and documents that live evidence-source wiring is DEFERRED to whichever future SD first fences a genuinely-reversible op-co component with metadata.switchon_action set (tracked via an explicit it.todo, not silently dropped).
acceptance_criteria: An SD with exec_boundary_hold=true and NO metadata.switchon_action behaves byte-identically to the pre-existing gate (manual park only, no gate evaluation, no decision packet fired)

An SD with metadata.switchon_action set to a NEVER-AUTO class (e.g. live-venture-deploy) never auto-clears the hold, regardless of any other metadata field including switchon_in_role — GUARDRAIL-1 parity

An SD with metadata.switchon_action set to a reversible, in-role, non-live-money, non-venture-commitment action with runSwitchOnPrechecks STUBBED green DOES auto-clear the hold and the PLAN-TO-EXEC handoff proceeds (unit-test-level proof; live precheck evidence-source wiring is an explicit, documented deferral — see FR-1 description)
GUARDRAIL-2: a test proves an irreversible/never-auto action is hard-stopped at this exact gate and a decision packet is dispatched — not an inert/advisory flag

functional_requirements.title: One-tap chairman decision-packet dispatcher (durable + idempotent)
functional_requirements.description: New module lib/switch-automation/switchon-decision-packet.js exporting notifySwitchOnDecisionPacket(supabase, {sdKey, action, reasons}). Always durably records via lib/chairman/record-pending-decision.mjs's recordPendingDecision({title, decisionType:'switchon_gate', context: {sd_key: sdKey, action, reasons}, blocking:true, raisedBy:'switchon-gate'}) — blocking:true triggers the EXISTING shouldAutoEscalate email path with zero new email-sending code. THEN best-effort attempts lib/chairman/sms-bridge.js's sendChairmanSmsQuestion(supabase, {decisionId, chairmanUserId: process.env.CHAIRMAN_USER_ID, chairmanEmail: process.env.CHAIRMAN_EMAIL, chairmanPhone: process.env.CHAIRMAN_PHONE, title, decisionType:'switchon_gate', context}) — this call already self-degrades to console-only when classifyConsequence() returns 'high' (the correct outcome for genuinely irreversible op-co actions) or when CHAIRMAN_PHONE/CHAIRMAN_USER_ID are unset (fail-soft: log and skip, never throw — the durable console record from the first call is authoritative regardless of SMS delivery). IDEMPOTENT (VALIDATION F3 fix — recordPendingDecision nests caller-supplied context under brief_data.context, it does NOT write a top-level sd_key column, so the dedup query MUST match the real write shape): before recording, query chairman_decisions for an existing status='pending' row with brief_data->>raised_by='switchon-gate' AND brief_data->context->>sd_key=sdKey AND brief_data->context->>action=action; if found, return {recorded:false, deduped:true, existingDecisionId} without inserting a duplicate or re-sending SMS — a PLAN-TO-EXEC retry loop must not spam the chairman on every poll.
acceptance_criteria: First call for a given (sdKey, action) creates exactly one chairman_decisions row and triggers the existing email escalation path
A second call for the SAME (sdKey, action) while the first decision is still pending is a no-op (deduped:true, zero new rows, zero new SMS/email sends)
SMS send is attempted but its failure/skip (missing env config, HIGH consequence, rate limit) never prevents the durable console record from being created

Once the chairman resolves the pending decision (status leaves 'pending'), a subsequent call for the same (sdKey, action) is treated as fresh (not deduped) — a resolved-then-re-triggered gate is not permanently silenced

functional_requirements.title: First concrete application: SD-FDBK-ENH-EHG-OPERATING-COMPANY-001-A
functional_requirements.description: One-time metadata migration on SD-FDBK-ENH-EHG-OPERATING-COMPANY-001-A (via a scripted one-off, not a DB schema migration): clear metadata.requires_human_action / metadata.do_not_auto_dispatch (SD's own fence text already states LEAD/PLAN may proceed), set metadata.exec_boundary_hold=true with metadata.exec_boundary_hold_reason preserving the original chairman-gated rationale, and stamp metadata.switchon_action='live-venture-deploy' (the first of -A's own 3 named irreversible actions; any NEVER_AUTO_CLASSES member produces the identical always-consequential outcome, so which one is tagged 'primary' does not change behavior) plus metadata.switchon_context noting the other 2 named actions (live-payment-account-creation, dns-mutation) for the decision packet's context field. VALIDATION F4: op-co-A's live metadata carries FOUR additional ad-hoc, pre-exec_boundary_hold-era markers that are inert for claim-eligibility (grepped: read by no live code path) but are exactly the kind of contradictory-fence 'misread fuel' the PRD's own Risk-#3 warns about — hold{class,reason,set_by,gated_phase}, do_not_auto_dispatch_exec, irreversible_exec_chairman_gated, do_not_auto_dispatch_reason. The one-off script must NOT silently leave these live: move them verbatim into a new metadata.switchon_migrated_from_legacy_fence archive object (preserving audit history) and unset the live top-level keys, so a future reader of op-co-A's metadata sees exactly one canonical hold shape (exec_boundary_hold), not five overlapping ones. Explicitly OUT of scope: -B (chairman-PARKED for demand-gating, an unrelated business-timing reason already once wrongly unfenced and re-fenced by the coordinator — touching it again risks repeating that exact documented mistake) and -C (depends on -B, inherits the same hold). This asymmetry is intentional and documented, not a coverage gap.
acceptance_criteria: SD-FDBK-ENH-EHG-OPERATING-COMPANY-001-A is claimable for LEAD/PLAN work immediately after this SD merges (requires_human_action/do_not_auto_dispatch cleared, exec_boundary_hold=true)

SD-FDBK-ENH-EHG-OPERATING-COMPANY-001-A's PLAN-TO-EXEC handoff, when attempted, is intercepted by FR-1's gate, evaluates as never-auto (consequential), and FR-2's decision packet fires exactly once per pending cycle — verified in a test with the real metadata shape, not a synthetic fixture
SD-FDBK-ENH-EHG-OPERATING-COMPANY-001-B and -C metadata are UNCHANGED by this SD (explicit negative test)

technical_requirements.title: No mutation of Child A/B/C contracts
technical_requirements.description: classifySwitchOn, authorizeSwitchOn, runSwitchOnPrechecks signatures and NEVER_AUTO_CLASSES are consumed as-is; this SD is purely a caller, never edits those modules' exported behavior.

technical_requirements.title: exec_boundary_hold claim-eligibility exclusion preserved
technical_requirements.description: Per the existing GUARDRAIL comments in lib/fleet/claim-eligibility.cjs, exec_boundary_hold must never be added to INELIGIBILITY_AXES or CLAIM_WRITE_FENCE_AXES — this SD's gate-wiring lives entirely inside the PLAN-TO-EXEC gate, not the claim path.

technical_requirements.title: Fail-closed on evaluator error
technical_requirements.description: Any error thrown by classifySwitchOn/authorizeSwitchOn/runSwitchOnPrechecks during gate evaluation must be caught and treated as a NEVER-AUTO/unknown verdict (fires the decision packet, WAITs) — never treated as authorized on ambiguity.

technical_requirements.title: No new email-sending code
technical_requirements.description: Chairman email escalation is entirely delegated to the existing recordPendingDecision + shouldAutoEscalate + escalateChairmanDecision pipeline (SD-LEO-INFRA-CHAIRMAN-DECISION-QUEUE-001) — this SD adds zero new spawn/email logic.

test_scenarios.scenario: EXEC_BOUNDARY_HOLD gate byte-identical behavior with no switchon_action
test_scenarios.expected: Existing manual-park test suite (exec-boundary-hold.test.js) passes unmodified

test_scenarios.scenario: Never-auto switchon_action never auto-clears, decision packet fires
test_scenarios.expected: Hold stays true, WAIT returned, notifySwitchOnDecisionPacket called exactly once

test_scenarios.scenario: Reversible, in-role switchon_action with runSwitchOnPrechecks STUBBED green auto-clears
test_scenarios.expected: exec_boundary_hold flips false, passed:true returned, no decision packet fired (live precheck evidence-source wiring is an explicit documented deferral, tracked via it.todo, not exercised by this test)

test_scenarios.scenario: Decision packet idempotency using the real brief_data.context nesting
test_scenarios.expected: Second call for the same pending (sdKey, action), queried via brief_data->context->>sd_key, is deduped; a resolved-then-retriggered call is not

test_scenarios.scenario: SMS send failure never blocks the durable console record
test_scenarios.expected: Mocked SMS throw/reject still returns recorded:true with the chairman_decisions row created
-A fence converted correctly; -B/-C metadata unchanged (negative test)

acceptance_criteria: GUARDRAIL-1 parity: a switchon_action matching any NEVER_AUTO_CLASSES entry never auto-proceeds through this gate, verified by an explicit test enumerating the shared list
GUARDRAIL-2: the gate is enforced in code at the real PLAN-TO-EXEC decision path (not an inert flag) with a passing hard-stop test — closes the parent's op-co-A EXEC-hardstop precedent gap
op-co-A is unfenced for LEAD/PLAN and correctly routes EXEC to a one-tap chairman decision packet instead of an indefinite silent park
op-co-B/-C are explicitly untouched, with the reason documented in code comments and the PR description

risks.risk: Unfencing -A for LEAD/PLAN causes a worker to build significant PLAN-phase artifacts (PRD) that then sit indefinitely at the EXEC boundary awaiting chairman ratification
risks.mitigation: This is the parent SD's explicit intent (unblock the reversible majority of the work, not the irreversible tail) and matches the plan's own stated goal — documented as expected, not a defect

risks.risk: CHAIRMAN_PHONE/CHAIRMAN_USER_ID env vars are unset in production, so the SMS half of the decision packet never fires
risks.mitigation: The console record + email escalation path (already wired, already production-tested) is the durable primary channel regardless; SMS is additive best-effort, not a hard dependency — flagged explicitly in the PR description as a follow-up config item, not a build blocker

risks.risk: A future coordinator or worker misreads exec_boundary_hold=true on op-co-A as 'fully unblocked' and attempts to force EXEC past the gate
risks.mitigation: The gate fails closed on any switch-on evaluator error and NEVER_AUTO_CLASSES always wins regardless of caller intent — no code path exists to force-authorize

consumers: Any future phase-scoped-fenced SD that stamps metadata.switchon_action
data_contracts: metadata.switchon_action / switchon_reversible / switchon_in_role / switchon_reversible_by_mechanism / switchon_is_live_money / switchon_is_venture_commitment / switchon_context on strategic_directives_v2 (no schema migration — existing JSONB metadata column)

metadata.switchon_migrated_from_legacy_fence (new archive key on op-co-A only, preserves the pre-existing hold/do_not_auto_dispatch_exec/irreversible_exec_chairman_gated/do_not_auto_dispatch_reason markers)
runtime_config: CHAIRMAN_PHONE (new, optional — SMS half degrades gracefully if unset)
observability_rollout: No feature flag — gate only activates per-SD via the opt-in metadata.switchon_action stamp, so existing exec_boundary_hold users are unaffected by default

system_architecture.summary: One new lib module (decision-packet dispatcher) + one modified existing gate file (additive branch, byte-identical fallback) + one one-off metadata script applying the gate to a real SD.

components.name: scripts/modules/handoff/executors/plan-to-exec/gates/exec-boundary-hold.js
components.change: MODIFIED (additive branch gated on metadata.switchon_action presence)

components.name: scripts/one-off/apply-switchon-gate-opco-a.mjs
components.change: NEW (one-shot metadata migration script, not a DB schema migration)

implementation_approach.summary: Build the decision-packet dispatcher first (independently testable), then wire it + the switch-on gate into exec-boundary-hold.js (with the existing manual-park suite as a regression guard), then apply to op-co-A last.
steps: Write lib/switch-automation/switchon-decision-packet.js with idempotency + fail-soft SMS, unit tests for all branches

Modify exec-boundary-hold.js to call authorizeSwitchOn + runSwitchOnPrechecks + the decision-packet dispatcher when switchon_action is present, keeping the existing manual-park path as the untouched fallback
Re-run the existing exec-boundary-hold.test.js suite unmodified to prove byte-identical fallback behavior
Add new tests for the switchon_action branch (never-auto WAIT+packet, reversible+green-prechecks auto-clear, evaluator-error fail-closed)
Write and run the one-off script applying the new fence shape to SD-FDBK-ENH-EHG-OPERATING-COMPANY-001-A, verify -B/-C untouched

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Run the PLAN-TO-EXEC handoff for a fixture SD with exec_boundary_hold=true and no switchon_action
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: WAIT verdict, identical to pre-SD behavior

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Run the PLAN-TO-EXEC handoff for a fixture SD with switchon_action='live-venture-deploy'
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: WAIT verdict, notifySwitchOnDecisionPacket fires once, a chairman_decisions row is created

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Run the PLAN-TO-EXEC handoff for a fixture SD with switchon_action set to a reversible class and all prechecks stubbed green
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: PASS verdict, exec_boundary_hold flips to false

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Inspect SD-FDBK-ENH-EHG-OPERATING-COMPANY-001-A's metadata after the one-off script runs
